methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $299M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $700M - $3.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
Electra is a Series B climate and energy technology company founded by Sandeep Nijhawan and Quoc Pham, having raised $299M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 9 comparable companies (1 public, 8 private), including same-sector, same-stage players such as KoBold Metals, Electric Hydrogen, and Amogy, all valued in Series C.

Two valuation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using the $299M total raised as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yields $568M. Peer-median analysis across the three primary comparables—valued at $700M, $1.0B, and $3.0B respectively—produces a median of $1.0B. The peer range spans $700M to $3.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in capital efficiency and technology maturity within the climate tech sector.

Triangulating these two methods produces a valuation range of $483M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $784M. This range reflects tension between stage-based methodology, which anchors to historical capital deployment, and peer benchmarking, which captures market pricing of comparable-stage climate tech ventures. Key uncertainties include unit economics relative to peers, differentiation in underlying technology, and sector-wide funding volatility in clean energy infrastructure.
